title: On Seeing Verses Written Upon Windows At Inns (Epigrams On Windows)
content:
The sage, who said he should be proud
        Of windows in his breast,[1]
    Because he ne'er a thought allow'd
        That might not be confest;
    His window scrawl'd by every rake,
        His breast again would cover,
    And fairly bid the devil take
        The diamond and the lover.
meaning:
Jonathan Swift's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On Seeing Verses Written Upon Windows At Inns (Epigrams On Windows)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
